programme.desc: New Roles for Vacant Homes Peter Barakan explores innovative methods for adding value to numerous vacant properties in Japan, which were once considered worthless.
programme.title: Japanology Plus Mini

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: Vanishing Tools The hosts explore vanishing tools in various crafts and their anonymous designs.
programme.title: Design X Stories

programme.desc: Genji Monogatari (The Tale of Genji) The host presents classical Japanese literature, in both original form and modern rendition, that conveys the worldview of ancient Japan.
programme.title: Story Land in Classical Japanese

programme.desc: A comprehensive compilation of insightful tales that cater to the interests of young viewers by incorporating elements of humor.
programme.title: Mitateru-Phose: Let's Change Perspective!

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: UBUME Experts embark on a journey to discover and explore the Yokai, the creatures responsible for creating strange phenomena, while tracing their stories in folklore.
programme.title: Yokai

programme.desc: Michinoku Coastal Trail Northern Section Kyle Card hikes along the 621 miles trail following the Pacific Coast in northern Japan, where he discovers local lifestyles, stunning nature, traces of the area's 2011 disaster and resilient people.
programme.title: Cinematic Journey

programme.desc: Doodles or Ukiyoe The filmmakers investigate what kind of artistic statement Utagawa Kuniyoshi tries to make in his series of doodles depicting the kabuki actors of the day.
programme.title: Ukiyoe Edo-Life

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: How to Survive an Earthquake Experts talk about technology that helped people survive the earthquake in Japan and share survival tips.
programme.title: BOSAI: Science That Can Save Your Life

programme.desc: Carving the Spirit of Fishermen Takeya Takayuki, a model maker, learns about the uniquely carved and decorated fishing boats used in the herring industry before the 1950s.
programme.title: Design Museum Japan

programme.desc: The World of Kabuki Choreography Choreographers create the movement and staging of a kabuki performance, contributing hugely to its appeal. Our guest, expert choreographer Fujima Kanjuro, talks about the intricacies of the job.
programme.title: Kabuki Kool

programme.desc: Japan's disaster relief; Asia news; business and global current events.
programme.title: NHK Newsline

programme.desc: The Deadly Escape Game of Refugees The number of refugees in the world reaches unprecedented heights, and they become pawns in a game with their lives at stake.
programme.title: On the Edge

programme.desc: Indonesian news and culture, in English
Controversial British officer T.E. Lawrence (Peter O'Toole) learns the culture of Arabs (Alec Guinness, Anthony Quinn) and unites their tribes against the Turks.
programme.title: Lawrence of Arabia

programme.desc: A New York dancer (Fred Astaire) grooms a chorus girl (Judy Garland) to be his new partner, falling in love along the way.
programme.title: Easter Parade

programme.desc: The sons (Humphrey Bogart, William Holden) of a Long Island tycoon become romantic rivals for the chauffeur's daughter (Audrey Hepburn).
programme.title: Sabrina

programme.desc: When a persistent posse threatens two outlaws' (Paul Newman, Robert Redford) romp through Wyoming, they decide to take their act to Bolivia.
programme.title: Butch Cassidy and the Sundance Kid

programme.desc: A student hooker (Annie Potts) in a van picks up a Las Vegas-bound teen (Mark Hamill) on the trail of a stolen Stingray.
programme.title: Corvette Summer

programme.desc: A heroin addict is caught between two Times Square narcotics agents and the pusher they are trying to bust.
programme.title: Born to Win

programme.desc: With an ambitious plan, a PR woman and a country-music bandleader turn a Texas truck driver into a rock 'n' roll star.
programme.title: Loving You

programme.desc: Corrupt Russian officials mistake a traveling buffoon (Danny Kaye) for the czar's dreaded inspector general.
programme.title: The Inspector General

programme.desc: A cattle baron (John Wayne) tries to tame his wife (Maureen O'Hara) amid a feud with settlers and trouble with Indians.
programme.title: McLintock!

programme.desc: Lovers (Lana Turner, Ezio Pinza) meet in Italy as an actress and a prince, then 12 years later as a movie star and a king.
programme.title: Mr. Imperium

programme.desc: Dagwood (Arthur Lake) brings home a borrowed Great Dane, which Blondie (Penny Singleton) enters in a dog show.
programme.title: Blondie in Society

programme.desc: Lawyer's (John Litel) daughter Nancy (Bonita Granville) and her boyfriend (Frankie Thomas) rescue an old woman from crooks.
programme.title: Nancy Drew, Detective

programme.desc: Kirk (William Shatner), Spock (Leonard Nimoy) and the Enterprise crew battle an old foe (Ricardo Montalban) who blames Kirk for the death of his wife.
programme.title: Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan

programme.desc: While a Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor looms, an Army sergeant (Burt Lancaster), a former boxer (Montgomery Clift) and an officer's wife (Deborah Kerr) become entangled with others at an Army base on Oahu.
programme.title: From Here to Eternity

programme.desc: An Indian (Burt Reynolds) resorts to killing to stop an outlaw (Aldo Sambrell) and his gang from killing.
programme.title: Navajo Joe

programme.desc: A cartoonist (Dean Martin) turns his babbling roommate's (Jerry Lewis) bizarre dreams into a hit comic strip read by spies.
programme.title: Artists and Models

programme.desc: A Harvard man (Robert Young) and his Colonial friend (Walter Brennan) join Rogers' (Spencer Tracy) Rangers in upstate New York.
programme.title: Northwest Passage

programme.desc: A bounty hunter (Glenn Ford) faces his son's killers with help from the son (Michael Burns) of an outlaw he has killed.
programme.title: Santee

programme.desc: A poor Chicago secretary (Diana Ross) takes the fast lane to fame as a model and a fashion designer.
programme.title: Mahogany

programme.desc: Railway police take action when kidnappers choose their busy station as the drop-off point for their ransom money.
programme.title: Union Station

programme.desc: A Broadway star does a show about life during World War I while his son does one in World War II to boost a soldier's morale.
programme.title: This Is the Army

programme.desc: A snooty socialite (Katharine Hepburn) fights with her ex-husband (Cary Grant) and flirts with a reporter (James Stewart).
programme.title: The Philadelphia Story

programme.desc: A barmaid, a Bostonian and an Indian (Rita Gam) flirt with an artist (Scott Brady) in 18th-century upstate New York.
programme.title: Mohawk

programme.desc: Based on the story by Louisa May Alcott. The four March sisters forge unbreakable emotional ties during the Civil War.
programme.title: Little Women

programme.desc: S05 E21 — Lucy and Tennessee Ernie Ford Lucy tries for the business of a millionaire country-music star.
programme.title: The Lucy Show

programme.desc: An astronaut and his team crash on a world ruled by intelligent, talking simians who treat humans like animals.
programme.title: Planet of the Apes

programme.desc: A paratrooper's (Dean Martin) goofy buddy (Jerry Lewis) joins him on base and makes a mockery of maneuvers.
programme.title: Jumping Jacks

programme.desc: Rejected by a young woman (Hayley Mills) in London, a scientist (John Hurt) retreats to Antarctica to study the penguin.
programme.title: Mr. Forbush and the Penguins

programme.desc: A Korean War hero's (Laurence Harvey) commanding officer (Frank Sinatra) discovers he and his platoon were brainwashed.
programme.title: The Manchurian Candidate

programme.desc: A pet deer changes a boy (Claude Jarman Jr.) and his parents (Gregory Peck, Jane Wyman), pioneering farmers in Florida after the Civil War.
programme.title: The Yearling

programme.desc: International swindlers are diverted from their sinister dealings when an explosion wrecks the ship they are aboard.
programme.title: Beat the Devil

programme.desc: After a laboratory courtship, the Polish scientist (Greer Garson) and her French husband (Walter Pidgeon) discover radium in 1898.
programme.title: Madame Curie

programme.desc: A couple (Dick Van Dyke, Debbie Reynolds) break up after 15 years of marriage, then get back together as the easy way out.
programme.title: Divorce American Style

programme.desc: A lovesick attorney (Raymond Burr) tries to trap a murder client (Angela Lansbury) he got acquitted by arranging for her to kill him.
programme.title: Please Murder Me

programme.desc: The frontiersman (Bruce Bennett) leads settlers to Kentucky, where he makes peace with a Shawnee chief (Lon Chaney).
programme.title: Daniel Boone, Trail Blazer

programme.desc: Stan (Stan Laurel) and Ollie (Oliver Hardy) join the Foreign Legion and wind up scheduled for a firing squad.
programme.title: The Flying Deuces

programme.desc: A racetrack tout (Bob Hope) has a month to pay back the sum a gangster (Lloyd Nolan) lost on a bad tip.
programme.title: The Lemon Drop Kid

programme.desc: An exile (John Richardson) from a hostile tribe woos a woman (Raquel Welch) from a gentle tribe amid monsters and an earthquake.
programme.title: One Million Years B.C.

programme.desc: Allied commandos (Gregory Peck, David Niven, Anthony Quinn) try to knock out a Nazi fortress over the Aegean.
programme.title: The Guns of Navarone

programme.desc: Cartoons
programme.title: Cartoons

programme.desc: S01 E20 — Blondie's Double Dagwood thinks Blondie is singing at a nightclub, after Mr. Dithers sees a woman who looks like her.
programme.title: Blondie

programme.desc: The queen of Egypt (Elizabeth Taylor) seduces Julius Caesar (Rex Harrison), but when he is killed, she uses Mark Antony (Richard Burton) as her new protector.
programme.title: Cleopatra

programme.desc: A modern-day blacksmith becomes the toast of King Arthur's court after being transported to sixth-century England.
programme.title: A Connecticut Yankee in King Arthur's Court

programme.desc: Superspy Derek Flint (James Coburn), agent of Z.O.W.I.E., stops a geothermal plot to control the world's weather.
programme.title: Our Man Flint

programme.desc: U.S. soldiers (Van Johnson, John Hodiak, Ricardo Montalban) from all over fight in the Battle of the Bulge.
programme.title: Battleground

programme.desc: A Greek partisan (Telly Savalas) helps Allied prisoners escape with ancient art from a German major's (Roger Moore) island prison.
programme.title: Escape to Athena
